title: Blechnum robustum
taxonomy: Blechnum robustum is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants that include ferns and their allies. The class Polypodiopsida is further divided into several orders, with Blechnaceae belonging to the order Blechnales. Blechnum robustum is a member of the genus Blechnum, which is a group of ferns commonly known as chain ferns. The classification of Blechnum robustum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns.

The plant's rhizomes and roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. intro: Blechnum robustum, also known as the robust water fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the Blechnaceae family. The robust water fern is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum robustum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
